title: Three-minute videos shift democratic understanding across 33 countries
imageAlt: Students in navy uniforms sit on a bench with smartphones, a soccer ball at their feet
summary: A large-scale experiment finds that brief online videos explaining democratic principles — rights, checks and balances, accountability — measurably strengthen support for democracy and reduce acceptance of authoritarian alternatives, even among the politically disengaged.

title: Trump AI Policy Framework Overrides Local Consent on Data Center Expansion
imageAlt: KD Chavez, executive director of Climate Justice Alliance, in black turtleneck against colorful abstract background
summary: New federal AI policy fast-tracks data center permits while stripping local governments of oversight — a test case for whether infrastructure governance flows from communities or concentrates upward with federal and corporate power.

title: Can ecological collapse be undone? China's Yangtze experiment offers an answer
imageAlt: Aerial view of the Yangtze River winding through terraced landscape
summary: A decade-long commercial fishing ban across the Yangtze basin — affecting an area the size of Mexico — shows early signs of reversing biodiversity loss. The scale of intervention required reveals what ecological restoration actually demands.

title: Kiss the Ground Grants $500K to 215 Farms, Shifting 73,000 Acres to Regeneration
imageAlt: Four farmers standing beside mulched garden beds with forest backdrop in Cazadero, California
summary: Direct grants to small-scale farmers reduced transition risk for regenerative practices across 73,000 US acres in 2025. Now the work shifts from capital deployment to storytelling — connecting eaters to the human systems behind soil governance.

title: The Bioplastics Trade-off: Climate Gains, Biodiversity Losses
imageAlt: Agricultural landscape showing crop cultivation for bioplastic feedstock production
summary: New lifecycle analysis reveals bioplastics reduce carbon but harm ecosystems more than fossil alternatives — a material governance challenge where the only path to climate targets involves reducing demand itself.

title: Water governance standard launches in Tokyo with emphasis on felt practice
imageAlt: Attendees gather for group photo at AWS Standard 3.0 launch in Tokyo
summary: The Alliance for Water Stewardship released Version 3.0 of its certification standard at Suntory's Tokyo headquarters, bringing stakeholder engagement and catchment-level planning to corporate water management across the Asia-Pacific region.

title: Carceral Education as Social Innovation: Stephanie Fanfan's Research
imageAlt: Stephanie Fanfan, PhD researcher studying carceral education and justice capital
summary: A PhD student at Learning Planet Institute examines how education within prisons shapes justice capital and institutional recognition — testing whether carceral systems can transform or merely replicate inequality.

title: EnergyNet and the Governance of Local Power
imageAlt: Illustrated figures and infrastructure representing local energy systems with solar panels and transmission towers
summary: Viable Cities explores how local energy systems can reshape urban climate action — not just as infrastructure, but as participatory governance. The EnergyNet model, tested in Lund, treats energy as a coordinated system involving households, property owners, and municipalities.

title: Global democracy returns to 1978 levels as autocratization accelerates
imageAlt: Protester holds sign reading 'Presidents Are Not Kings' at demonstration in urban street
summary: V-Dem's annual assessment finds 74% of humanity now lives under autocracy, with 44 countries actively autocratizing. The US loses liberal democracy status as freedom of expression and legislative constraints deteriorate worldwide.

title: Phage research as governance practice: learning from living systems
imageAlt: Portrait of Milena Milovanović, PhD researcher studying bacteriophage systems at FIRE Doctoral School
summary: A doctoral researcher at FIRE examines bacteriophages to understand life's fundamental coupling of form and function — a question with implications for how we design adaptive, living governance systems.

title: Portrait: Chloé Anna Höllerer enters FIRE's doctoral cohort
imageAlt: Chloé Anna Höllerer, first-year PhD student at FIRE Doctoral School
summary: A new PhD student joins the FIRE Doctoral School at Learning Planet Institute, bringing a background in agronomy and molecular biology to questions of research and learning systems. Another researcher entering the work of governance experimentation.

title: Water stewardship standard revised to meet catchment-scale governance demands
imageAlt: Aerial view of winding river through green landscape illustrating watershed governance at catchment scale
summary: The Alliance for Water Stewardship releases Version 3.0 of its certification framework, refining how corporations engage with watershed governance beyond efficiency metrics — a shift toward catchment-scale accountability as water becomes boardroom risk.

title: Small states, strength in numbers: rebuilding human rights multilateralism
imageAlt: Speaker at World Economic Forum Davos podium addressing audience, blue branding visible
summary: As great powers fragment the UN human rights system, a coalition of 90 countries — half from the global south — is testing whether one-country-one-vote can still anchor planetary governance in shared rules rather than fortress politics.

title: Rewilding as governance in Ukraine's Danube Delta
imageAlt: Water buffalo grazing in wetland grasses of Ukraine's Danube Delta rewilding area
summary: A new documentary shows how rewilding in Ukraine's Danube Delta operates as both ecological restoration and social healing — restoring natural processes while offering veterans and communities a lived practice of renewal amid war.

title: Tharaka's River of Life: eco-cultural mapping as governance practice
imageAlt: Tharaka spiritual leader in ceremonial headdress sits in dugout canoe by riverbank, Kenya
summary: The Tharaka community in Kenya adapts Amazonian mapping methods to create Life Plans — participatory governance tools that weave seed sovereignty, sacred site protection, and customary law into a holistic vision of territorial stewardship.

title: Two rewilding initiatives join pan-European network, expanding into Greece and Türkiye
imageAlt: Brown bear wearing tracking collar in green scrubland, part of CALLISTO coexistence monitoring
summary: The European Rewilding Network adds CALLISTO and Marine Rewilding Türkiye to its roster of over 100 initiatives — one focused on human-carnivore coexistence, the other on coastal ecosystem recovery. Both embed local communities in restoration governance.

title: Global Challenges Foundation opens 2026 call for governance reform proposals
summary: The Swedish foundation behind the New Shape Prize has announced a new round of funding for proposals that address gaps in planetary-scale governance. This cycle emphasises prototyping over theory — a shift that aligns closely with The Garden's approach.

title: Decommodifying land in Philadelphia — governance from the ground up
imageAlt: Row houses in an urban neighborhood with mixed commercial and residential buildings
summary: Community land trusts in Kensington and beyond are testing whether neighborhoods can govern their own futures against displacement. It's hyperlocal democratic innovation with a clear thesis: ownership structures are governance structures.

title: Agroecology Europe seeks administration officer to anchor European food transition
imageAlt: Agroecology Europe logo on green background with hiring announcement
summary: The Brussels-based network coordinating Europe's agroecological transition is hiring operations capacity — a quiet signal of organizational maturity in a movement linking farmers, researchers, and activists across borders.

title: Nestlé certifies all bottling sites under AWS Standard — but what counts as stewardship?
imageAlt: Aerial view of forested watershed with river running through valley
summary: Nestlé's achievement of AWS certification across 39 bottling sites raises deeper questions about how corporations govern shared water resources — and whether certification frameworks can transform extraction into genuine stewardship.

title: Tanzania trains public officials in water stewardship as Dar es Salaam rations supply
imageAlt: Participants in a training session discussing water stewardship in Tanzania
summary: As drought forces weekly water rationing in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania launches water stewardship training for 30+ public officials — an experiment in building trust-based governance between state agencies and private actors around shared catchments.

title: U.S. Senate Passes Housing Act Centering Shared Equity Models
imageAlt: Community land trust housing development with shared green space
summary: Bipartisan legislation elevates community land trusts and shared equity programs to federal policy — testing whether ownership models that prioritize stewardship over speculation can operate at scale.

title: Testing Resilience Infrastructure in Cities Rebuilt from Disaster
imageAlt: Research team gathered at traditional Japanese building during Tokyo project kickoff
summary: A three-year transnational project examines how nature-based infrastructure performs in post-disaster cities across Japan, Sweden, and Poland — treating urban recovery as a testing ground for ecological governance under stress.

title: Rewilding loan tests whether nature recovery can anchor rural economies
imageAlt: Three people view terraced vineyards and rolling hills in Portugal's Greater Côa Valley from a dirt road
summary: A €40,000 loan from Rewilding Europe Capital enables a Portuguese nature tourism company to expand accommodation in the Greater Côa Valley — testing whether financial tools can align ecological restoration with economic viability in depopulating rural regions.

title: Testing governance in soil: herbs, certification, and practiced resilience
imageAlt: Text logo reading '11 with Evan' in dark gray sans-serif font against white background
summary: Kyle Bliffert's journey through the supplement industry traces a shift from theoretical wellness to practiced ecological stewardship. At Gaia Herbs' 270-acre Regenerative Organic Certified farm, governance becomes tangible — tested by hurricanes and fire alike.

title: Metagov publishes framework for interoperable governance across digital and physical spaces
summary: The research collective has released a working paper proposing standards for governance tool interoperability — enabling rules and processes to flow between online communities, DAOs, and physical assemblies.

title: Democracy Without Borders releases brochure on global cooperation
imageAlt: Bronze sculpture of a revolver with its barrel tied in a knot, displayed outside the UN building
summary: Democracy Without Borders has published a new brochure examining pathways to reimagine global cooperation. The resource synthesizes emerging proposals for multilateral reform and democratic participation beyond borders.

title: Agroecology Europe seeks communicator to bridge research and political practice
imageAlt: Agroecology Europe logo with blue and green leaf-like circular design on yellow background
summary: The Brussels-based network — 200 members spanning farmers, academics, and activists — is hiring a communications officer to strengthen its work placing agroecological transition on Europe's governance agenda.

title: New Jersey's PB Seeds program shows how advocacy training spreads democratic practice
imageAlt: Diverse group of PB Seeds participants gathered around table in working session with papers and materials
summary: A structured cohort model for participatory budgeting advocacy is generating new processes across New Jersey — including youth-led climate budgeting in Newark. Evaluations reveal what participants need to become effective democratic innovators.

title: A New Index Sorts Regimes by How Power Actually Works
imageAlt: Presentation slide showing 'Tyranny Tracker Coming Soon' at Oslo Freedom Forum event
summary: The Human Rights Foundation's Tyranny Tracker distinguishes democracies from hybrid and authoritarian regimes using qualitative thresholds — not aggregated scores — to capture the moment when democratic systems break down.

title: U.S. Congress Considers Its Largest Housing Bill in Decades
summary: The Senate is weighing the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which would expand community land trusts and shared equity models — governance experiments that treat housing as stewardship rather than speculation.

title: Climate Justice Alliance Links Militarism to Climate Crisis in Iran Strike Statement
imageAlt: Illustrated poster showing firearms entangled in flowering vines and birds, reading 'Divest from wars, invest in a regenerative future'
summary: As U.S. and Israeli forces strike Iran, the Climate Justice Alliance frames military action as ecological governance failure — the U.S. military remains the world's largest institutional emitter while resource extraction drives geopolitical conflict.

title: Agroecology's quiet spread across 33 European countries
imageAlt: Horses grazing along a rural road bordered by green pastures and forest in Europe
summary: A five-year mapping project documents hundreds of agroecological initiatives across Europe — from farming practices to living labs — showing how food system transformation happens through distributed experimentation rather than top-down policy.

title: Corruption perceptions worsen as civic space contracts worldwide
imageAlt: Protesters holding banners and flags at a large street demonstration demanding anti-corruption action
summary: Transparency International reports global corruption perceptions have fallen to their lowest level in over a decade, with shrinking civic space a common factor in declining scores. The trend reveals how governance legitimacy depends on openness.

title: Scottish rewilding lodge joins pan-European practitioner network
imageAlt: Aerial view of Ballintean Mountain Lodge courtyard surrounded by autumn forest and Highland mountains
summary: Ballintean Mountain Lodge, a 30-year rewilding experiment in the Cairngorms, joins the European Rewilding Network — a move that underscores how ecological restoration increasingly operates through distributed governance: peer learning, knowledge exchange, and practice-led networks rather than top-down mandates.

title: Three Climate Justice Groups Challenge EPA's Erosion of Tribal and State Water Authority
imageAlt: River or stream flowing through landscape, representing watershed governance
summary: A joint comment letter opposes EPA proposals that would weaken Section 401 of the Clean Water Act — the mechanism that allows states and Tribal Nations to condition or deny federal permits based on water quality standards.

title: Warren, Merkley bill targets tax breaks for corporate landlords
imageAlt: Multi-unit residential housing with front porches and fencing in suburban development
summary: Senate Democrats propose ending tax benefits for firms that own 450,000 single-family homes and 2.2 million apartments — treating housing as extractive asset class rather than commons. A test of whether governance can reclaim shelter from financialization.

title: Gaia Foundation Seeks Regional Coordinators for UK Seed Sovereignty Network
imageAlt: Group of seed sovereignty practitioners gathered in garden setting with buildings and autumn trees
summary: Two new coordinators will work across southern and western England to strengthen grassroots seed networks — governance infrastructure for food systems that roots sovereignty in regional practice and Indigenous knowledge.

title: Gen-Z protests erupt globally — but their political direction remains uncertain
imageAlt: Youth protesters gather in street demonstration, some seated, some standing, waving national flag
summary: Youth-led uprisings across 11+ countries share grievances about inequality and corruption, but don't point toward a clear democratic renewal. The question isn't whether young people can mobilize — it's whether existing governance systems can absorb their demands.

title: Massachusetts defends inclusionary zoning as Fifth Amendment suits spread
imageAlt: Residential buildings on a tree-lined urban street in Cambridge, Massachusetts
summary: A developer's constitutional challenge to Cambridge's affordable housing rules could reshape how 1,000+ U.S. inclusionary zoning policies function — testing whether local governance can require shared stewardship of urban space.

title: Östersund tests governance through material reuse — and saves 10 million kronor
imageAlt: Interior of Materialbanken showing shelves of colorful reused materials and craft supplies
summary: A Swedish municipality embeds circular economy into budget processes and public institutions, treating reuse not as a side project but as core governance infrastructure. The results: 250 tons of CO2 avoided, millions saved, and a model others can copy.

title: Fifth International Rights of Nature Tribunal convenes with expanded mandate
summary: The Global Alliance for the Rights of Nature has convened its fifth tribunal, this time with an expanded mandate covering ocean governance and AI-mediated environmental harm — territory that bridges Garden and Spaceship orientations.

title: EURIP opens applications for interdisciplinary research on planetary challenges
imageAlt: Group discussion in modern collaborative space with participants seated on floor cushions
summary: Learning Planet Institute's Graduate School in Paris seeks students willing to cross disciplinary boundaries to address systemic challenges — a model that tests whether academic structures can adapt to planetary-scale problems.

title: Forests as rain-makers: governance beyond biodiversity
imageAlt: Dense forest canopy showing the vegetation that generates terrestrial evaporation and rainfall
summary: New satellite research reveals that 40% of corn and 60% of wheat depend on land-based rainfall — unstable moisture from forests, wetlands, and soil that agricultural expansion is actively destroying.

title: Paul Hawken on Curiosity, Soil, and Fire-Triggered Succession
imageAlt: White text reading '11 WITH Evan' on dark background, interview series branding
summary: The author of Regeneration and founder of Project Drawdown reflects on what regenerative agriculture actually means — not as branding exercise, but as practice that must be felt, verified, and embodied through direct relationship with land and living systems.

title: The perverse arithmetic of coral reef recovery
imageAlt: Coral reef ecosystem with fish, representing potential food security and marine governance challenges
summary: Coral reefs could sustainably yield 9,000 additional meals per square kilometer — but only if communities dependent on fishing accept decades of reduced catch. A stark example of governance as temporal negotiation.

title: What makes a rewilding image worth seeing — and judging
imageAlt: Photographer in dry suit holding large Atlantic sturgeon in shallow river water
summary: As the Rewilding Europe Award deadline approaches, photographer Jon A. Juárez reflects on what makes rewilding imagery matter: not just aesthetics, but the governance relationships between people, science, and returning ecosystems.

title: Digital Twins of Earth: Testing Governance Before the Crisis Arrives
imageAlt: Landslide probability map of Italy showing risk zones in purple and orange based on precipitation data
summary: ESA's Digital Twin Earth program creates real-time planetary simulations — not just to model floods or fire, but to stress-test governance responses before disaster strikes. It's scenario planning made operational, fed by satellite data and constrained by physics.

title: Pastoralists as Land Stewards: Mobility, Tenure, and Rangeland Governance
imageAlt: Shepherd in red vest tending mixed flock of sheep across golden rangeland with mountains beyond
summary: A coalition webinar ahead of UNCCD COP17 reframes pastoralism as regenerative governance — not degradation driver. The challenge: translating centuries of mobile land stewardship into policy that recognizes rights to territory and movement, not just plots.

title: Three reports link extreme wealth concentration to democratic erosion
imageAlt: Aerial view showing stark contrast between wealthy neighborhood and dense informal settlement
summary: New assessments from Oxfam, a G20 expert committee, and the World Inequality Lab find billionaire wealth has grown 81% since 2020 while democratic institutions weaken — evidence that governance systems cannot be separated from economic architecture.

title: Housing Network Expands Capacity Through Nonprofit Learning Partnership
imageAlt: Community members collaborating at a table with documents and planning materials
summary: Grounded Solutions Network sponsors Nonprofit Quarterly's Leading Edge program, connecting community land trusts and housing justice organizations with management training amid federal funding pressures — infrastructure for the infrastructure.

title: Housing Network Joins NPQ to Strengthen Nonprofit Governance Infrastructure
imageAlt: Professionals collaborating in workshop or meeting setting
summary: Grounded Solutions Network partners with Nonprofit Quarterly to give housing justice organizations access to governance training and peer networks — recognizing that transformative systems work requires strengthened organizational capacity.

title: Lake Kartal: wetland governance tested at war's edge
imageAlt: Aerial view of Lake Kartal in Ukraine's Danube Delta, showing reconnected waterways and floodplain
summary: In Ukraine's Danube Delta, six years of restoration has reconnected 18,000 hectares of floodplain to the river — a reminder that ecological governance means reshaping flows, not just protecting boundaries, even under the strain of invasion.

title: François Taddei on Building a Learning Planet
imageAlt: Podcast cover art showing lightning bolt logo and text 'Courant Alternatif' over blurred background
summary: The Learning Planet Institute's co-founder joins a podcast exploring educational models as governance infrastructure — treating learning not as preparation for citizenship but as citizenship itself.

title: IPBES Report Maps the $7 Trillion Gap Between Economic Growth and Ecological Reality
imageAlt: IPBES Chair at the Manchester plenary session with nameplate and presentation screen showing February 2026 date
summary: The first fast-track business-biodiversity assessment reveals a stark asymmetry: $7.3 trillion in nature-harming flows versus $220 billion for restoration — a gap that threatens economic stability and requires governance innovation at the intersection of markets and ecosystems.

title: What makes a rewilding image powerful? A photographer's lens on conservation storytelling
imageAlt: Photographer framing shot with hands, woman with glasses centered through finger frame
summary: As Europe's wildlife photography competition opens submissions, judge Viktoria Pezzei argues for images that show human hands in ecological work — not pristine wilderness, but the lived practice of restoration.

title: Coexistence guidance equips European mayors to govern alongside returning wildlife
imageAlt: Shepherd with walking stick and goat on mountainside, overlooking valley with village below
summary: A new toolkit launched by Rewilding Europe offers municipal leaders practical frameworks for human-wildlife coexistence — recognizing local governance as the crucial layer where ecological recovery becomes lived experience.

title: EPA Revokes Endangerment Finding as Justice Networks Mobilize Resistance
imageAlt: Coalition members at environmental justice rally with signs demanding EPA accountability
summary: The Trump EPA's revocation of the 2009 Endangerment Finding — the scientific basis for climate regulation — triggers coordinated resistance from environmental justice networks representing millions of frontline communities.

title: Habitat Seattle doubles down on community land trusts and debt remediation
imageAlt: Habitat for Humanity Seattle staff gathered together on a residential construction build day
summary: A Habitat affiliate in Washington state has pioneered a debt remediation program while scaling community land trust homeownership — testing new mechanisms for equitable access to housing that lasts generations.

title: Youth governance at the UN: from symbolic participation to structural influence
imageAlt: United Nations Youth Office logo with UN emblem on gradient background
summary: Student delegates at a UN Youth Office dialogue explore what meaningful participation looks like — not merely consultation, but structural influence in institutional decision-making. The event tested frameworks for youth engagement as governance practice.

title: Youth governance at the UN: from symbolic presence to structural voice
imageAlt: Large diverse group of young participants gathered for UN Youth Compass dialogue with virtual attendees on screen
summary: The UN Youth Compass dialogue tested whether intergenerational governance can move beyond consultation theater. Student participants assessed what meaningful engagement actually requires — not just seats at tables, but power to reshape them.

title: Sweden expands city climate coalition beyond pilot group
imageAlt: Swedish city street with mixed historic and modern buildings
summary: Viable Cities opens new call to expand beyond its 48-city Climate Neutral Cities 2030 initiative. The expansion tests whether collaborative governance models can scale — a recurring question in transition practice.

title: The Work of Re-membering: Communities Attune to the Rhythms of Land
imageAlt: Three generations gather under shade, drawing and mapping together with colored pencils
summary: From Kenyan clans reviving biocultural knowledge to Antarctic rights advocacy, communities worldwide are rekindling what Thomas Berry called the 'Great Conversation' — governance as relationship, not extraction.

title: Normandy's PRELE initiative joins European rewilding network
imageAlt: Muddy woodland path winds through dense green undergrowth and bare trees in Normandy forest
summary: A regional French programme letting forests and wetlands recover without human interference joins a network of 105 rewilding initiatives — advancing a governance model where ecosystems manage themselves.

title: Hope as Educational Infrastructure
summary: François Taddei frames hope not as sentiment but as educational architecture — a necessary foundation for governance systems that must adapt to planetary-scale challenges while remaining rooted in human capacity for learning.

title: Philadelphia neighborhood tests perpetual trust as anti-displacement tool
imageAlt: Commercial storefront on Kensington Avenue with community mural visible
summary: The Kensington Corridor Trust removes 30+ properties from speculative markets through a perpetual purpose trust governed by 32,000 local residents — testing whether collective ownership can anchor neighborhoods against displacement.

title: Agroecology versus Regenerative Agriculture: A Question of Power
imageAlt: Red and pink poppies bloom in a polytunnel greenhouse at Einion's Garden, Wales
summary: Belgian-Welsh farmer Ann Owen argues that regenerative agriculture serves corporate interests while agroecology builds practitioner power. The distinction matters: one treats farming as optimization, the other as transformative practice rooted in social and ecological balance.

title: UNESCO launches climate learning experience linking purpose discovery to collective action
imageAlt: UNESCO logo on light blue gradient background
summary: MOOD — Meaningful Open Opportunities for Discovery — brings 100+ participants into a UN-certified learning experience that frames climate action as both personal exploration and governance practice, testing how structured discovery might scale.

title: Two risk reports map a fracturing governance landscape
imageAlt: Helicopter with water bucket silhouetted against massive wildfire flames and smoke
summary: The Global Challenges Foundation and World Economic Forum release assessments showing seven of nine planetary boundaries breached, rising geoeconomic confrontation, and institutional erosion — all pointing to what GCF calls the need for governance that recognizes 'planetary commons cannot be negotiated with.'

title: Ukrainian Universities as Test Sites for Climate-Resilient Reconstruction
imageAlt: Video conference screen showing UVICITIES Transition Lab Forum II presentation with participants
summary: UniCities transitions from project to partnership, positioning Ukrainian universities as laboratories for integrated climate transition and post-war recovery — a model for resilience-centered governance under extreme constraint.

title: When global cooperation fails to deliver, solidarity declines with it
imageAlt: Aerial view of pedestrians crossing a zebra crossing, showing diverse people moving in different directions
summary: New polling across 31 countries shows falling support for international institutions — not because people reject cooperation, but because they don't see it working. The gap reveals something fundamental about governance at any scale.

title: ETHAKA Mozambique: Testing Agroforestry as Governance Infrastructure
imageAlt: Three people tending soil in an agroforestry plot with banana trees and mixed crops, Mozambique
summary: A 3.5-year project in Mozambique embedded agroforestry systems into public institutions and rural communities — showing how regenerative agriculture becomes a form of governance when it restructures land use, diet, and policy in tandem.

title: Not Only Larp brings governance simulation to the European Parliament
summary: The Italian design studio ran a three-day political LARP inside the European Parliament, with MEPs and citizens role-playing a planetary governance crisis. The event drew attention to experiential methods for democratic innovation.

title: Migrant Justice as Climate Practice: Communities Organize Against Displacement
imageAlt: Father lifts joyful child at sunset over green hills and water, with text 'Justice for Migrants'
summary: The Climate Justice Alliance frames immigration enforcement as inseparable from climate displacement, calling for mutual aid networks and direct action as federal raids intensify across the U.S.

title: The Federal Home Loan Banks as Infrastructure for Shared Ownership
imageAlt: Chart showing U.S. Census Bureau data on housing patterns across Federal Home Loan Bank regions
summary: Eleven regional cooperative banks allocate billions for affordable housing through mandated programs — including community land trusts. A case study in how existing financial infrastructure can be steered toward stewardship models.

title: AI for Sustainability Finds Its Test Ground in Taipei
imageAlt: Conference participants at ICAISS2025 holding organizational signs and sustainability symbols in auditorium
summary: Researchers, policymakers, and industry practitioners gathered in Taipei to explore how artificial intelligence can serve planetary resilience — examining not just technical potential but governance frameworks for equitable deployment.

title: How communication work gets done: an internship at Viable Cities
imageAlt: Four people sitting around a conference table with laptops, one person holding up a star-shaped object
summary: Vendela Karlsson spent autumn 2025 embedded in Viable Cities' communications team, translating urban climate transition work into accessible formats. A small case study in how governance initiatives become legible — and who does that work.

title: Learning Planet Academy pivots education from excellence to planetary service
imageAlt: Audience members raising hands during interactive session in bright modern learning space
summary: The Learning Planet Institute launches a new academy designed to move beyond traditional credentials toward co-constructed learning pathways that address climate urgency and ecological anxiety — a pedagogical experiment in governance education.

title: Mariestad: Testing governance through climate investment data
imageAlt: Two people discussing map of Mariestad at outdoor information booth with bicycle visible
summary: A Swedish mid-sized city maps who pays for climate transition and who benefits — finding that while citizens and industry carry costs, the municipality's role is creating conditions for transformation, not funding it.

title: Viable Cities opens internships in climate communications
imageAlt: Four people in an office pointing upward at a starfish decoration, laptops and coffee on table
summary: Sweden's Viable Cities seeks communications interns to help 29 cities navigate climate neutrality transitions. The work touches a persistent challenge: how to make systemic change legible and compelling to publics who must ultimately enact it.

title: River Reuss Heads to Referendum: Switzerland Tests Legal Rights for Water
imageAlt: Reuss Initiative members hold blue flags reading 'JA' outside Lucerne parliamentary building
summary: A citizen initiative in Lucerne canton has gathered 5,460 signatures to grant legal personhood to the River Reuss — launching a constitutional process that will test whether democratic systems can recognize nature as a rights-bearing entity.

title: Fish-inspired filter captures 99% of microplastics without clogging
imageAlt: Close-up of fish gills showing the intricate filtering structures that inspired the microplastic filter design
summary: Researchers at Bonn University developed a self-cleaning filter modeled on fish gills that captures microplastics from washing machines — addressing a governance gap where household infrastructure meets ocean pollution.

title: One scientist, 1,460 rice varieties, and the architecture of food sovereignty
imageAlt: Hands holding golden indigenous rice grains — part of Dr Debal Deb's 1,460-variety seed bank
summary: Dr Debal Deb has spent 30 years conserving indigenous rice from remote Indian tribes — an act of ecological stewardship that doubles as governance practice, returning seed sovereignty to farmers after decades of corporate monoculture.

title: Sinking Carbon: A Proposal to Store Boreal Wood in the Arctic Seafloor
imageAlt: Conceptual visualization of timber submerged in dark Arctic waters
summary: Researchers propose harvesting boreal forest timber and sinking it in the oxygen-poor Arctic Ocean — a carbon removal method that sidesteps infrastructure costs but raises questions about who governs such planetary-scale interventions.

title: European bison return to the Iberian Highlands — and to the policy question
imageAlt: European bison standing in snowy landscape, facing camera directly
summary: Nine European bison have been released in Spain's Iberian Highlands as part of a cross-European study testing how keystone species can restore degraded landscapes — and whether rewilding can offer viable development pathways for depopulating rural communities.

title: Luleå's transition exhibition: governance as public pedagogy
imageAlt: Visitors explore climate transition exhibition in Luleå shopping center, January 2026
summary: A Swedish industrial city places climate transition in a shopping mall — treating governance not as policy abstraction but as something residents encounter while buying groceries. The experiment tests whether municipal legitimacy can be built through visibility.

title: EU-Mercosur: a test case for whether agroecology can move from niche to policy
imageAlt: Cattle grazing among rocks and olive trees in a Mediterranean agroecological landscape
summary: As the EU-Mercosur trade agreement advances despite farmer protests, Agroecology Europe argues both continents must accelerate agroecological policy — not as alternative, but as systemic governance strategy for food, climate, and rural resilience.

title: 18 Groups Challenge EPA's Narrowing of Clean Water Protections
imageAlt: Wetland ecosystem with standing water and vegetation
summary: Climate Justice Alliance and 17 EJ organizations oppose EPA's proposed redefinition of protected waters — a case study in how regulatory rollbacks shift environmental harm to frontline communities while weakening federal accountability.

title: US Withdraws from 65+ International Bodies — Including All Climate Frameworks
imageAlt: Empty seats in an international conference hall representing absent participation in global governance
summary: The Trump administration withdrew from the UNFCCC and 65+ other international bodies, making the US the only nation outside the foundational climate treaty. A stress test for multilateral governance in an age of unilateral retreat.

title: GARN Opens Internships in Rights of Nature Movement Infrastructure
imageAlt: Illustration of bee and flowers in a jar with text listing GARN internship areas
summary: The Global Alliance for the Rights of Nature seeks interns across communications, tribunal support, and organizing — roles that build the operational capacity behind legal ecosystems protection and governance experimentation.

title: Climate Justice Alliance maps data center impacts, trains ground organizers
imageAlt: Industrial infrastructure and community organizing materials
summary: A frontline coalition created resources on AI infrastructure's environmental costs and trained 10 organizers in Memphis to challenge data center development — governance as ground-level strategy, not abstract policy.

title: Five Years of Agroforestry Practice in Costa Rica: Jungle Project's Farmer-First Model
imageAlt: Five-year data table showing growth in family farms, training sessions, participants, and plants established
summary: Over 20 hectares and 18,000 plants later, Jungle Project demonstrates how governance begins with land stewardship — training 22 farmers in regenerative agroforestry while building the market infrastructure to sustain it.

title: Agroecology Europe's Year of Movement-Building Amid Collapse and Commitment
imageAlt: Frost-covered winter landscape with bare trees and golden sunrise, representing seasonal transition
summary: As political systems retreat from environmental pledges, Europe's agroecology network grows to 450+ youth members and convenes gatherings that bridge science, practice, and policy — testing governance models that treat food systems as living democratic experiments.
